name: ai-summary
title: AI Summary
description: Reviewable structured summary with configurable sections, per-section status, and per-section provenance.

props.name: sections
props.description: { id, heading, content?, status?, statusDetail?, provenance?, uncertainty? }. Order is the caller's.

props.name: sections[].status
props.description: generated = model prose; empty = reviewed, nothing found; omitted = never produced; verbatim = copied unchanged from the record.

props.name: sections[].statusDetail
props.description: Shown in place of content for empty/omitted sections — say what was checked, or what was not read.

props.name: sections[].provenance
props.description: Per-section sources — AiCitation elements or an AiEvidencePanel. A generated or empty section without provenance renders as unsourced and says so.

props.name: sections[].uncertainty
props.description: What is uncertain or contested inside this section.

props.name: onCopySection
props.description: Caller copies. The component never touches the clipboard.

props.name: onInsertSection
props.description: Hands a section to the caller for insertion. The component never writes to a record.
metadata, confidence, limitations, evidence, and actions pass through to AiAnswerCard.

clinicalNotes: A summary's most dangerous property is that it reads as complete. Three failures look identical in ordinary prose and are separated here by section status: a section the model wrote (`generated`), a section it reviewed and found nothing in (`empty` — "no safeguarding concern recorded"), and a section it never produced at all (`omitted` — the medication list was not read). An omitted section is not a negative finding, and the component says so in place of the content rather than leaving a blank a reader will fill in with an assumption. Provenance is per-section for the same reason: a summary can be sourced in one paragraph and invented in the next, so a `generated` or `empty` section with no provenance is drawn with a warning rule and labelled unsourced instead of quietly inheriting the card's overall citations. Nothing here writes to the chart — `onCopySection` and `onInsertSection` hand the section back to the caller, and the caller owns the record, the audit trail, and the decision to keep the text.
notes: Composes AiAnswerCard; per-section provenance slots accept AiCitation / AiEvidencePanel. Section variants exported as aiSummarySectionVariants.
